mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling from an already-low level, the discount rate on future earnings declines and equity valuation multiples expand. This should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month, with the effect strongest while policy is still easing and before inflation re-accelerates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 50 bps over 3 months
failure_condition: If triggered episodes do not show higher 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the sign turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable or falling, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate captures a deteriorating labor market and falling household income expectations, which depresses equity demand and increases recession odds. A sharp monthly increase in UNRATE should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as markets reprice growth risk immediately.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-threshold episodes do not produce below-baseline 5-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate for negative returns is not above 55% in triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized contraction in retail sales, housing starts, and industrial production signals broad real-economy demand collapse. The joint weakness should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next month because earnings revisions and credit-risk premia both worsen together.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined weakness pattern does not outperform each single proxy in explaining 20-day SPX direction, or if triggered joint-decline episodes fail to produce negative average forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A steep monthly drop in industrial production is a direct signal of supply-side shutdowns and disrupted output. When INDPRO falls abruptly, corporate revenue and margin expectations should weaken, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls by more than 2.0 points month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if large monthly INDPRO drops are not followed by materially lower 20-day SPX returns than the unconditional average, or if the sign is positive across repeated shock episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated housing starts relative to prior months signals stronger cyclical demand, better construction activity, and improved household balance-sheet confidence. That should support S&P 500 returns over the next quarter through higher growth expectations and better cyclicals performance.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month housing upswings do not precede above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears in more than one rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE identifies labor-market recovery. That combination should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because payroll gains validate future consumption and reduce recession pricing.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down condition does not improve prediction versus either series alone, or if 20-day SPX returns are not positive in a majority of matched instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate regime, further increases in FEDFUNDS tighten financial conditions and raise the discount rate applied to equities. This should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter, especially after the policy rate crosses a restrictive threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-rate episodes do not show below-average 60-day SPX returns, or if return direction remains positive after the threshold is crossed in a sustained sample.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising CPIAUCSL and falling RSAFS indicates inflationary pressure with weakening nominal demand, a stagflation-like mix that hurts equity margins and valuation simultaneously. Under that joint pattern, S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-up and sales-down months do not jointly predict lower 20-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if the combined condition fails to produce a negative average forward return.
